context_lines: Acts 20:17 should be read within the flow of its chapter and the larger argument of Acts.
common_interpretation_lines: One group of men. Called elders. Made overseers. Told to shepherd. Three words, one office, one function — caring for people, not ruling a rank. Peter makes the same connection:

Elder, shepherd, overseer — again the same men, the same task, described three ways. Not a hierarchy. A job, seen from three angles: age and maturity (elder), responsibility for watching over souls (overseer), and the actual daily work of tending people (shepherd).
